answers.answer: The existing test file at frontend/src/__tests__/DiscussionThread.test.tsx does NOT exist. No DiscussionThread test file exists anywhere in the codebase. answers.answer: The project convention places ALL test files in frontend/src/__tests__/ (currently 9 test files: ErrorBoundary, format, Portfolio, MarketDetail, EventTradePanel, CreateMarketForm, api-client, MarketList, System). The test file should follow this convention and be placed at frontend/src/__tests__/DiscussionThread.test.tsx, NOT at the co-located path frontend/src/features/market/DiscussionThread.test.tsx. The existing vitest config has no special include/exclude patterns — it uses default discovery which would find tests in either location, but consistency matters.
evidence: Glob frontend/src/__tests__/*.test.*: 9 files all in __tests__/ directory
Glob frontend/src/**/*.test.*: same 9 files, no tests elsewhere
frontend/vitest.config.ts: setupFiles points to __tests__/setup.ts, no testMatch override
frontend/src/__tests__/helpers.tsx: shared test helpers in __tests__/ directory
answers.uncertainty: The task description says co-located path but convention says __tests__/. Following convention is the right call but noting the discrepancy.

assumption_checks.impact: Critical — the test file does not exist at all. We need to create frontend/src/__tests__/DiscussionThread.test.tsx from scratch with tests covering all 8 specified scenarios: rendering comment list, empty state, loading state, error state, comment submission, disabled composer for non-active markets, character count display, disabled when no session.

The prior child task (bayes-comments-001-execute-impl-child-1) only implemented backend tests (14 new tests in tests/test_bayes_market_api.py). No frontend tests were created. This task must create the frontend tests from scratch.

There are no DiscussionThread tests to pass or fail. The tests need to be written. The testing infrastructure is set up (vitest + jsdom + @testing-library/react + @testing-library/jest-dom) and working (9 existing test files), so the foundation is solid.

implementation_notes.mocking_strategy: Mock @/lib/api/client (same pattern as MarketDetail.test.tsx and CreateMarketForm.test.tsx). The component uses useMarketComments and usePostMarketComment hooks which call api.getMarketComments and api.submitMarketComment respectively. Mock these at the api level.
implementation_notes.test_helpers: Use renderWithProviders from ./helpers which wraps with QueryClientProvider, SessionProvider, and MemoryRouter
scenarios_to_cover: 1. Renders comment list correctly (mock getMarketComments with comments array)
2. Shows empty state ('No comments yet.') when comments array is empty
3. Shows loading state ('Loading discussion...') before data resolves
4. Shows error state when query fails
5. Submits comment via composer form (type in textarea, click Post Comment)
6. Disables composer when market.status !== 'active' (shows read-only note)
8. Disables composer when no session configured (shows 'Set your Account ID' note)

key_mock_data_shapes.market: { id, title, description, variableId, status, outcomes, marginals, liquidity, volume, created_at, expires_at }
key_mock_data_shapes.comments_response: { marketId, comments: MarketComment[], pagination: { fromSeq, limit, returned, nextFromSeq }, meta }
key_mock_data_shapes.comment: { commentId, marketId, seq, accountId, body, createdAt }

implementation_notes.session_handling: SessionProvider reads from localStorage. To test 'no session' state, don't set localStorage. To test 'configured' state, set localStorage with bayes-session key before rendering.
